preview_text:
The model favors Canada at 41%, with Switzerland a 28% underdog. The gap of 13 percentage points reflects a meaningful but not decisive edge for the Canadian side in the forecast.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 31%, nearly level with Canada's win probability. This clustering of outcomes in the 28–41% range indicates a competitive match unlikely to be settled by a dominant performance; the result remains genuinely open across all three possibilities.

Model and market diverge noticeably here. The sportsbook consensus backs Switzerland at 39% and Canada at 30%—a reversal of the model's ranking, with a gap of roughly 11 percentage points on each side. Polymarket prices align closely with the books. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the disagreement reflects genuine uncertainty about which team holds the true edge.
